paper_metadata.title: CTRL: A Conditional Transformer Language Model for Controllable Generation
paper_metadata.abstract: Large-scale language models show promising text generation capabilities, but users cannot easily control particular aspects of the generated text. We release CTRL, a 1.63 billion-parameter conditional transformer language model, trained to condition on control codes that govern style, content, and task-specific behavior. Control codes were derived from structure that naturally co-occurs with raw text, preserving the advantages of unsupervised learning while providing more explicit control over text generation. These codes also allow CTRL to predict which parts of the training data are most likely given a sequence. This provides a potential method for analyzing large amounts of data via model-based source attribution. We have released multiple full-sized, pretrained versions of CTRL at https://github.com/salesforce/ctrl.

extraction_data.innovation: CTRL introduces a conditional transformer language model that enables controllable text generation by injecting control codes into the input sequence. These control codes, which can represent domains, styles, or other attributes, are prepended to sequences and guide the model's output without requiring prompt engineering.
extraction_data.pretraining_corpus: Wikipedia (En, De, Es, Fr), Project Gutenberg, 45 subreddits, OpenWebText, news data (Hermann et al., 2015; Barrault et al., 2019; Sandhaus, 2008; Grusky et al., 2018), Amazon Reviews (McAuley et al., 2015), Europarl and UN data from WMT (En-De, En-Es, En-Fr), ELI5 question-answer pairs, MRQA shared task (SQuAD, NewsQA, TriviaQA, SearchQA, HotpotQA, Natural Questions)
extraction_data.finetuning_task: Controllable text generation via control codes
extraction_data.extension: Introduces a set of conditional control codes that allow fine-grained, task-specific control over text generation, enabling zero-shot code mixing and predictable variation in output.
extraction_data.research_problem: Controllable text generation in language models
extraction_data.application: Text generation with explicit control over style, domain, topic, entities, and task behavior; source attribution via model-based analysis of training data
extraction_data.paper_title: CTRL: A Conditional Transformer Language Model for Controllable Generation
